behaviors.description: Command segmentation treats newline-separated statements as separate segments, not one monolithic block.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is the segmentation logic itself (string/statement parsing), with no required module handoff, OS/network/subprocess, or model output dependence. Higher levels were considered and rejected: behavior text only requires segmentation correctness, not persistence or cross-hook interaction.
behaviors.test_description: Given a multiline shell snippet with newline-separated assignments and commands, assert the segmenter returns multiple ordered segments and does not collapse into one block.

behaviors.description: A multi-line shell block with variable assignments followed by `gh pr create` is recognized as a PR-create trigger.
behaviors.justification: Incident failure appeared at module boundary: segmentation + trigger classification together mis-labeled the block as `not_a_trigger`. Unit tests of either module alone can miss this handoff bug. Rejected System: behavior text is about recognition/classification of command text, not real subprocess/OS behavior. Rejected Agentic/Workflow: no AI/ML decision path.
behaviors.test_description: Wire real segmenter and trigger detector together; feed a multiline block ending in `gh pr create`; assert final classification is PR-create trigger.

behaviors.description: A similar multi-line block that ends with `gh issue create` is not incorrectly treated as a PR-create trigger.
behaviors.justification: This is the negative boundary case for the same cross-module path (segmentation + trigger classification). Real failure risk is misclassification after multiline parsing and command-type discrimination. Rejected System: no requirement for real `gh` execution or OS effects in behavior text. Rejected Agentic/Workflow: deterministic rule-based classification.
behaviors.test_description: Using integrated segmentation+classification path, input multiline assignments plus trailing `gh issue create`; assert classification is not PR-create trigger.

behaviors.description: When a PR-create trigger is detected, the hook can reconstruct and persist the PR identifier needed by the review loop state.
behaviors.justification: Needs interaction between trigger result, PR-id reconstruction logic, and persistence layer (local file/state writer). A pure unit on one function can miss schema/field wiring errors across modules. Rejected System: behavior does not require real network/subprocess; local filesystem persistence is sufficient under Integration definition. Rejected Agentic/Workflow: no model output dependence.
behaviors.test_description: Run hook path with a detected PR-create event fixture, verify PR identifier is reconstructed and written to state storage with expected fields.

behaviors.description: After PR-create detection, the review gate state is written in a form that the pre-tool enforcement hook can read and enforce.
behaviors.justification: Explicit cross-module handoff/order/state propagation: PostToolUse writes state, PreToolUse reads/enforces it. This is exactly an integration boundary. Rejected System: behavior text requires compatibility between hooks via state format, not real subprocess/network/OS primitives beyond normal local file I/O. Rejected Agentic/Workflow: no AI/ML decisions.
behaviors.test_description: Execute PostToolUse write path, then invoke PreToolUse read/enforcement path against that written state; assert enforcement triggers as expected with no schema mismatch.
